panels.description: Prometheus scrape target health for the sakana job (GET /api/metrics). 0 = process gone or host unreachable (monitoring.md, Target down).
panels.title: Backend target (up)

panels.description: tako_db_ok: 1 if the single-writer SQLite DB answers SELECT 1 at scrape time. 0 = the whole backend is effectively down (monitoring.md, DB unreachable). NOTE: lastNotNull holds the last reported value if the process dies, so this panel is only valid while 'Backend target (up)' is green -- process/host death is the up rule's and the Edge probes' job.
panels.title: Database (tako_db_ok)

panels.description: Share of 5xx responses over all responses (5m rate). Alert threshold 1% with a 30-requests-per-5m minimum-traffic guard (monitoring.md, Backend 5xx ratio - page).
panels.title: 5xx response ratio (5m)

panels.description: Request rate by route template, from starlette_requests_total (~13 templates total; topk dropped so legend membership stays stable across steps).
panels.title: Request rate by path

panels.description: Running backend version (tako_build_info{version} == 1; the displayed name is the version label). Deploy/restart markers on all panels come from the 'Deploys / restarts' annotation (changes of process_start_time_seconds).
panels.title: Backend version (tako_build_info)

panels.description: Live state of every provisioned alert rule (incl. pending and NoData). The public dashboard's honest answer to 'is anything firing right now?'.
panels.title: Alert state (all provisioned rules)

panels.description: p95 of starlette_requests_processing_time_seconds across API paths, excluding the self-scrape (path_template=/api/metrics dominates raw request counts and would otherwise be most of what this measures). SLO budget: < 150 ms (monitoring.md SLOs).
panels.title: API request duration p95

panels.description: Share of 4xx responses on POST /api/telemetry -- the single pipe all fleet visibility flows through. A sustained rise means beacons are being rejected (e.g. schema drift against the backend's extra=forbid BeaconMetrics) and the fleet view is silently flatlining.
panels.title: Telemetry pipeline 4xx ratio (/api/telemetry)

panels.description: Resident memory by scraped job (sakana backend, prometheus). On a 2 GB no-swap droplet, RSS creep ends with the OOM killer -- pair with the Host row's memory panel.
panels.title: Process RSS by job

panels.description: Cost of one GET /api/metrics collector pass (every scrape runs the SQLite scan). The early-warning series for telemetry-table growth -- sustained growth here is the leading indicator for the documented Postgres-migration trigger. Live baseline: ~14 ms avg, ~75 ms max.
panels.title: Collector scrape cost (scrape_duration_seconds)

panels.description: Registered users (including guests) and the guest subset. Point-in-time DB counts.
panels.title: Users

panels.description: Reviews per unique client_id, p50 and p95 (tako_reviews_per_client_*). Engagement depth. Note: the only un-windowed percentile family on this dashboard (all-time, not 7d).
panels.title: Reviews per client

panels.description: Total persisted ReviewSession rows over time (tako_reviews is a point-in-time DB count, not a counter; users can delete reviews, so it may decrease).
panels.title: Saved reviews trend

panels.description: Funnel stage totals (all-time DB counts), in funnel order: visit -> probe -> load -> generation -> saved. load and generation count ok=true beacons only; visit comes from funnel_stage events. Retention caveat: saved is never pruned while beacon stages prune at 90d, so all-time conversion can exceed 100% after day 90 -- the 24h funnel below is the honest recent view.
panels.title: Funnel events by stage (all-time)

panels.description: Funnel stage totals over the trailing 24h (tako_funnel_events_24h, computed by the backend collector), in funnel order: visit -> probe -> load -> generation -> saved. load and generation count ok=true beacons only. Prune-immune within its window -- the honest conversion view.
panels.title: Funnel events by stage (24h)

panels.description: Documented SLO: >= 95% of completed generations end in a persisted review (monitoring.md SLOs, saved / generation). Denominator clamped to 1 to guard div-by-zero. Caveat: all-time counts with mixed retention (saved never pruned, generation pruned at 90d) can push this above 100% after day 90.
panels.title: Saved / generation ratio (SLO >= 95%)

panels.description: Registered users by UI language preference (en | ja | other). The Japanese-first product thesis, with data. tako_users_by_ui_language{ui_language}.
panels.title: Users by UI language

panels.description: Saved reviews grouped by (allowlisted) language label. tako_reviews_by_language{language}.
panels.title: Reviews by language

panels.description: Saved reviews grouped by review_mode. tako_reviews_by_mode{review_mode}.
panels.title: Reviews by mode

panels.description: Feedback rows grouped by rating. tako_feedback{rating}. An empty panel means no feedback has been submitted yet -- not a pipeline failure.
panels.title: Feedback by rating

panels.description: GitHub OAuth attempts per second by outcome (1h rate of tako_auth_attempts_total, the app's only true Counter). A failure spike means OAuth misconfiguration (callback URL, client secret) or abuse.
panels.title: Auth attempts by outcome (1h rate)

panels.description: Lifetime fraction of webgpu_probe beacons reporting WebGPU support (cumulative DB counts). Adoption context, NOT an incident signal -- it barely moves during an incident; the 24h companion below is the one that moves.
panels.title: WebGPU support ratio (all-time)

panels.description: Share of webgpu_probe beacons in the trailing 24h reporting support (delta-windowed; clamp_min guards the 90d prune's non-monotonicity). Goes blank rather than 0% when there was no probe traffic in the window. This is the panel that moves during a browser-release incident.
panels.title: WebGPU support ratio (24h)

panels.description: Telemetry events carrying an error_kind (probe, load and generation failures; 'cancelled' is excluded -- a user cancel is not an error). tako_webgpu_errors{error_kind} is a cumulative DB count. Empty = no errors ever reported (good).
panels.title: WebGPU errors by error_kind

panels.description: Cumulative model_load beacon outcomes: attempts vs ok=true vs ok=false (point-in-time DB counts). attempts > success + failure = malformed or cancelled beacons.
panels.title: Model load: attempts / success / failure

panels.description: 24h-windowed model-load failure fraction (clamp_min'd deltas: the 90d prune makes the cumulative gauges non-monotonic). Shown from the first attempt in the window -- read low-volume values with care; the alert rule additionally requires >= 3 attempts before it can fire. error_kind='cancelled' is excluded from the failure count. Replaces the previous all-time ratio, which a single pre-TSDB failure pinned red at 50% forever.
panels.title: Model-load failure ratio (24h)

panels.description: Cumulative user cancellations (error_kind='cancelled' beacons): model download cancels and generation cancels. Excluded from failure ratios and webgpu_errors -- a cancel is not an error -- but a rising cancel count flags impatience with the ~1 GB download or slow generations.
panels.title: User cancellations (download / generation)

panels.description: webgpu_probe beacons by normalized device_class (collector-side normalization with a 64-series cap). The addressable-fleet view: which hardware the demo actually reaches.
panels.title: WebGPU probes by device class

panels.description: Model load duration percentiles per device_class and cache_hit (true | false | unknown), 7-day windowed gauges computed by the backend collector. The cache_hit split keeps instant warm (cached) loads from masking cold ~1 GB download regressions.

defaults.noValue: No data yet - awaiting fleet traffic

panels.title: Model load duration p50/p95/p99 by device_class and cache_hit (7d window)

panels.description: Client-side time to first token percentiles per device_class (7d windowed gauges). Device-bound, not a server signal.

defaults.noValue: No data yet - awaiting fleet traffic

panels.title: TTFT p50/p95/p99 by device_class (7d window)

panels.description: Client-side end-to-end review latency percentiles per device_class (7d windowed gauges).

defaults.noValue: No data yet - awaiting fleet traffic

panels.title: E2E latency p50/p95/p99 by device_class (7d window)

panels.description: Client-side decode speed percentiles per device_class (7d windowed gauges). Higher is better.

defaults.noValue: No data yet - awaiting fleet traffic

panels.title: Decode speed p50/p95/p99 by device_class (tok/s, 7d window)

panels.description: node_memory_MemAvailable_bytes. 2 GB droplet with NO swap: the page alert fires below 200 MB for 10m, after which the OOM killer is the next stop.
panels.title: Host memory available

panels.description: Available fraction per filesystem: root and the SQLite block volume /mnt/sakana_data (a full volume corrupts the single-writer SQLite DB -- the architecture's canonical killer). Ticket alert < 15%, page < 7%.
panels.title: Disk available % (/ and /mnt/sakana_data)

panels.description: 1-minute load average. Single vCPU droplet: sustained values above 1 mean CPU saturation.
panels.title: Host load (1m)

panels.description: Blackbox http_2xx probes of the public origin (/ and /api/health) -- what a visitor experiences through Caddy and TLS. Page alert when 0 for 2m; the absence of the series also pages (noDataState=Alerting).
panels.title: Public origin probes (probe_success)

panels.description: Days until the Let's Encrypt certificate expires, from the blackbox probe. Caddy renews ~30 days out; the ticket alert fires under 14 days (renewal has then been failing for weeks -- check for an orange-clouded Cloudflare record breaking the ACME challenge).
panels.title: TLS cert expiry (days)

panels.description: End-to-end blackbox probe duration per target. The probe originates on the droplet itself, so this measures Caddy + app turnaround, not visitor RTT from Japan.
panels.title: Probe duration by target
